notes: The engineering layer preserves the verse's closing function. The three properties are presented as the doctrinal close of the imperishable block; the therefore-clause is the operational instruction. Crucially, the engineering layer does NOT distort the verse into 'never feel anything about deprecated code' — both falsifiability and counter-example explicitly carve out the case where grief is information (pointing at unwritten specs) and where the deprecated work IS the spec (constraint documents, API guidelines). The reverse-translate test (analog → verse) yields a stance compatible with the imperishable doctrine: knowing the layer your work sits at, mourning at the wrong layer is misplaced, but spec-loss is not the wrong layer. The verse's anti-grief instruction is preserved as specifically a layer-error instruction, not a general affect-suppression. PASS.
